content:
FINAL IVX QA CLOSEOUT — RESOLVE ALL 8 BLOCKERS, DEPLOY, VERIFY, CERTIFY

Current status:

FINAL STATUS = QA IN PROGRESS

The audit reports that most systems pass, but 8 blockers remain.

Do not create another summary.
Do not create a new project.
Do not stop after identifying blockers.
Execute every machine-completable action now, deploy the fixes, verify production, and request owner action only for the true owner-only steps.

==================================================
CURRENT BLOCKERS TO CLOSE
==================================================

1. CloudFront invalidation permission
2. Android real-device QA
3. www redirect
4. 10 unpushed local commits
5. iOS app not built
6. Analytics not configured
7. Landing deployment CORS wildcard
8. Autonomous dashboard export missing

==================================================
PHASE 1 — RECONCILE ALL SOURCE CODE FIRST
==================================================

Before any deployment, verify the complete source-control state.

Run and return raw evidence for:

- git status
- git branch --show-current
- git rev-parse HEAD
- git log --oneline -20
- git fetch origin
- git rev-parse origin/main
- git log origin/main..HEAD --oneline
- git diff --stat origin/main..HEAD

For the reported 10 unpushed commits:

1. List every commit SHA.
2. List the commit message.
3. List files changed.
4. Explain whether each commit is required for production.
5. Remove accidental/generated artifacts from Git if necessary.
6. Run tests before pushing.
7. Push all approved commits to the IVX-owned GitHub repository.
8. Verify GitHub HEAD matches local HEAD.
9. Do not force-push unless required and explicitly justified.
10. Do not delete valid production history.

Required result:

LOCAL_HEAD = GITHUB_HEAD

If push fails, return the exact Git command and exact provider error.

Do not claim synchronization without proof.

==================================================
PHASE 2 — RUN COMPLETE BUILD AND TEST SUITE
==================================================

After source reconciliation, run:

- dependency installation
- TypeScript
- lint
- unit tests
- integration tests
- backend route tests
- landing production build
- chat production build
- Expo Doctor
- Android export/build validation
- iOS project validation

Required result:

- zero TypeScript errors
- zero critical lint errors
- zero failing required tests
- no missing production environment variables
- no secret values exposed in logs or bundles

Return:

CHECK | COMMAND | RESULT | PASS/FAIL | EVIDENCE

==================================================
PHASE 3 — FIX CLOUDFRONT INVALIDATION
==================================================

First verify whether the existing AWS credential can perform:

cloudfront:CreateInvalidation

Execute the real request and capture:

- IAM principal ARN
- CloudFront distribution ID
- AWS error code
- HTTP status
- denied action
- request ID

If permission exists:

1. Create an invalidation for:
   /*
2. Wait for completion.
3. Verify the public landing page serves the latest assets.
4. Return the invalidation ID and completion status.

If permission is denied:

Create the minimum required IAM policy recommendation:

{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "cloudfront:CreateInvalidation",
        "cloudfront:GetInvalidation",
        "cloudfront:GetDistribution"
      ],
      "Resource": "arn:aws:cloudfront::<AWS_ACCOUNT_ID>:distribution/<DISTRIBUTION_ID>"
    }
  ]
}

Send an owner-action notification containing:

- exact principal ARN
- exact denied action
- exact distribution ID
- exact policy required
- trace ID

After permission is granted:

- verify automatically
- create invalidation
- resume automatically
- do not require the owner to type “continue”

==================================================
PHASE 4 — FIX WWW REDIRECT
==================================================

Required behavior:

https://www.ivxholding.com
→ HTTP 301 or 308
→ https://ivxholding.com

Current HTTP 200 behavior is not accepted.

Audit:

- Route 53 records
- CloudFront alternate domain names
- TLS certificate
- CloudFront behaviors
- S3 origin type
- redirect function or edge function
- canonical tags
- sitemap URLs

Implement one correct production solution:

OPTION A:
Use an S3 static website redirect origin behind CloudFront.

OPTION B:
Use CloudFront Function or Lambda@Edge to issue the redirect.

Requirements:

- HTTPS works
- no redirect loop
- path preserved
- query string preserved
- exact Location header
- canonical domain remains ivxholding.com
- no duplicate indexed site

Test:

- /
- /properties
- /reels
- /register
- URL with query parameters

Return actual response headers.

==================================================
PHASE 5 — FIX LANDING DEPLOYMENT CORS
==================================================

Remove wildcard CORS from production landing-deployment and related administrative endpoints.

Allowed origins must include only the required IVX domains:

- https://ivxholding.com
- https://www.ivxholding.com
- https://chat.ivxholding.com

Include localhost only in non-production development configuration.

Verify:

- allowed origin receives correct Access-Control-Allow-Origin
- unapproved origin is rejected or receives no CORS permission
- credentials are never used with wildcard origin
- OPTIONS preflight works
- public read-only APIs remain available where required

Search the full codebase for:

- Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *
- cors({ origin: "*" })
- wildcard CORS configuration

Return all fixed files and live headers.

==================================================
PHASE 6 — CONFIGURE ANALYTICS
==================================================

Audit the analytics system before creating new integrations.

Check whether these already exist:

- Google Analytics 4
- Google Tag Manager
- Meta Pixel
- LinkedIn Insight Tag
- TikTok Pixel
- UTM capture
- registration conversion events
- reel engagement events
- property-view events
- investor-lead events

Do not duplicate tags.

Implement or complete:

- page_view
- view_property
- view_deal
- reel_view
- reel_complete
- reel_like
- reel_comment
- reel_share
- registration_start
- registration_complete
- login
- lead_submit
- investor_interest
- owner_login_success

Requirements:

- production-only IDs from existing variables
- consent behavior where applicable
- no private data in analytics payloads
- no passwords, tokens, full financial data, or sensitive KYC data
- UTM parameters stored with lead/member source
- duplicate event prevention

Return browser/network evidence proving events fire.

If analytics IDs are genuinely missing, identify the exact missing variable names and send one owner-action request.

==================================================
PHASE 7 — COMPLETE AUTONOMOUS DASHBOARD EXPORTS
==================================================

Add export capabilities to the existing Autonomous Dashboard.

Do not create a duplicate dashboard.

Required exports:

- CSV
- PDF
- JSON

Export filters must respect:

- date range
- agent
- department
- category
- status
- investor
- property
- deployment

Required reports:

1. Daily activities 1–100+
2. Agent performance
3. Code and commits
4. Deployments
5. Investors
6. Buyers and CRM
7. Failures and retries
8. Owner action requests
9. Evidence ledger
10. Executive summary

Requirements:

- owner-only authorization
- no secrets in exports
- investor privacy enforced
- timestamps and trace IDs included
- exports generated from real database records
- no fabricated activity
- large exports handled without crashing
- mobile download works
- desktop download works

Return live screenshots and sample redacted export evidence.

==================================================
PHASE 8 — BUILD AND VERIFY IOS
==================================================

First determine the real status of the reported iOS project.

Audit:

- ios project directory
- bundle identifier
- signing configuration
- provisioning requirements
- native dependencies
- compile errors
- Expo/EAS iOS profile
- previous build history
- TestFlight status

If this is an Expo-managed iOS build:

1. Validate Expo config.
2. Validate runtimeVersion.
3. Validate bundle identifier.
4. Create a fresh iOS build using the approved GitHub SHA.
5. Return the EAS build ID and status.

If this is a separate Swift project:

1. Run project validation.
2. Fix compile errors.
3. confirm source ownership.
4. Document signing requirements.
5. Build when signing credentials are available.

Do not claim the iOS app is complete unless a real build succeeds.

If Apple signing or account approval is the only blocker, send one precise owner-action notification with the exact requirement.

==================================================
PHASE 9 — REBUILD ANDROID FROM FINAL SHA
==================================================

Build a fresh Android APK from the final GitHub commit after all code changes are pushed.

Requirements:

- approved GitHub SHA
- correct Expo project ID
- correct Android package
- incremented versionCode
- correct runtimeVersion
- clean build cache
- embedded JavaScript bundle
- no fatal OTA dependency
- release signing
- installable APK

Return:

- GitHub SHA
- EAS/local build ID
- APK file or URL
- versionName
- versionCode
- runtimeVersion
- package name
- SHA-256 checksum
- build timestamp

Do not reuse an old APK built before the final fixes.

==================================================
PHASE 10 — AUTOMATED PRODUCTION REGRESSION
==================================================

Run automated QA against the live production environment.

BACKEND

Test all required routes:

- /health
- /readiness
- /version
- /diagnostics
- /api/reels
- /api/reels/:id
- /api/properties
- /api/properties/:id
- /api/deals
- /api/deals/:id
- /api/members/authoritative-count
- /api/metrics/authoritative-count
- /api/trpc/waitlist.getStats
- registration
- login
- owner login
- Admin HQ members
- autonomous dashboard
- notification endpoints
- chat rooms
- chat messages

LANDING PAGE

Test:

- Android Chrome viewport
- iPhone viewport
- tablet viewport
- desktop Chrome
- Safari-compatible viewport

Verify:

- no page jumping
- no scroll reset
- no white banner
- no black sections
- no horizontal overflow
- no broken media
- no stale assets
- no critical console errors
- no required request failures
- all CTAs work
- mobile navigation works
- www redirect works
- canonical URL correct

REELS

Verify:

- exactly one active player
- maximum three mounted players
- play
- pause
- mute
- swipe
- like
- comment
- share
- save
- no duplicate audio
- failed reel does not crash the feed
- same media IDs on app and landing page

REGISTRATION

Create one controlled QA member and verify:

- Supabase Auth
- canonical member row
- Members module
- Admin HQ
- same user ID
- no duplicate
- cleanup or QA label

OWNER

Verify:

- owner login
- invalid password rejection
- non-owner rejection
- Admin HQ
- Members
- Investors
- CRM
- Variables
- Owner AI
- Chat
- Autonomous Dashboard
- logout
- protected routes

==================================================
PHASE 11 — ANDROID REAL-DEVICE ACCEPTANCE
==================================================

This is the only task allowed to require a physical Android device.

Before requesting owner testing, all previous phases must pass.

Provide one final APK and a maximum five-step owner checklist:

1. Install and open APK.
2. Complete three cold starts.
3. Open Reels and perform 50 consecutive swipes.
4. Use chat and owner login, then background/foreground the app.
5. Run the app for 30 minutes using Wi-Fi and cellular.

Also request:

- one screen recording
- screenshots of app home, reels, chat, and owner login
- crash screenshot if any
- acceptance result

Do not require the owner to run developer commands.

For adb logcat, provide a separate optional technician procedure only if a crash occurs.

When the owner submits evidence:

- attach it to the active task
- analyze automatically
- repair any failure
- rebuild
- redeploy
- notify the owner automatically
- do not create a disconnected task

==================================================
PHASE 12 — AUTONOMOUS OWNER NOTIFICATIONS
==================================================

Send owner notifications only when necessary.

Events:

- OWNER_ACTION_REQUIRED
- DEPLOYMENT_COMPLETED
- DEPLOYMENT_FAILED_AFTER_RETRIES
- DEVICE_ACCEPTANCE_READY
- CRITICAL_REGRESSION_FOUND
- FINAL_QA_COMPLETED

Every owner-action notification must include:

- task ID
- trace ID
- exact blocker
- exact provider error
- action required
- resume status
- deep link to the dashboard item

After the owner completes the action:

- verify automatically
- resume automatically
- do not require “continue”

==================================================
PHASE 13 — FINAL DEPLOYMENT ALIGNMENT
==================================================

Verify all release components.

Return:

COMPONENT | SHA | DEPLOYMENT/BUILD ID | TIMESTAMP | LIVE URL | PASS/FAIL

Include:

- Local Git
- GitHub
- Render backend
- Landing page
- Chat frontend
- Supabase migrations
- Autonomous dashboard
- Android APK
- iOS build
- CloudFront configuration
- Analytics configuration

Rules:

- no local-only commits
- no stale GitHub
- no stale production service
- no old APK
- no unverified build
- no unresolved critical or high-severity security issues

==================================================
PHASE 14 — FINAL EVIDENCE PACKAGE
==================================================

Return one final table:

ITEM | BEFORE | ACTION EXECUTED | FINAL RESULT | PASS/FAIL | EVIDENCE

Include:

1. Local SHA
2. GitHub SHA
3. Unpushed commits
4. Backend SHA
5. Frontend SHA
6. Chat SHA
7. Deployment IDs
8. CloudFront invalidation
9. www redirect
10. CORS
11. Analytics
12. Dashboard exports
13. API routes
14. Landing QA
15. Reels QA
16. Registration
17. Member sync
18. Owner login
19. Admin HQ
20. Autonomous dashboard
21. Push notifications
22. Android APK
23. Android automated QA
24. Android real-device acceptance
25. iOS build
26. Security
27. Performance
28. Screenshots
29. Screen recordings
30. Remaining blockers

Also provide:

- final production release SHA
- release version
- release timestamp
- rollback commit
- recovery instructions
- final owner acceptance status

==================================================
FINAL VERDICT RULE
==================================================

Do not report:

COMPLETE
CERTIFIED
PRODUCTION READY
FULLY AUTONOMOUS
NO BLOCKERS

until all required evidence proves:

- all approved commits are pushed
- GitHub and production are aligned
- CloudFront invalidation succeeds
- www redirect works
- wildcard production CORS is removed
- analytics works
- dashboard exports work
- Android APK is built from final SHA
- Android real-device acceptance passes
- iOS status is accurately resolved
- registration reaches Admin HQ
- owner login works
- reels do not crash
- security has zero critical/high findings
- final screenshots and recordings are attached

If a genuine owner-only action remains, report:

FINAL STATUS = OWNER ACTION REQUIRED

and include only the exact verified owner actions.

Otherwise continue autonomously until:

FINAL STATUS = CERTIFIED
